mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals tightening monetary policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS rises by > 0.5pp in 3 months, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate above 9.0% indicates labor market weakness, reducing consumer spending and corporate earnings visibility, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0%, or 5d return volatility drops below 1.2% in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP (GDPC1) and consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) signals strong demand pressures and inflation, prompting central bank intervention that destabilizes equity markets, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60d returns in > 55% of cases where GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL both exceed their 4-month highs, or the correlation between GDPC1 and CPIAUCSL drops below 0.6 in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) indicates weakening supply-side capacity, creating deflationary pressures that reduce corporate margins and drive negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines by > 1.5%, or the 20d return volatility falls below 1.0% in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 227 (CPIAUCSL) triggers monetary policy tightening expectations, increasing bond yields and reducing equity risk premiums, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 227, or the 20d return correlation with CPIAUCSL drops below -0.3.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) reflects robust labor market conditions, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings, which predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of instances where PAYEMS rises by > 100k, or the 5d return volatility exceeds 1.5% in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer demand, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20d returns in > 50% of cases where HOUST and RSAFS both exceed their 2-month highs, or the correlation between HOUST and RSAFS drops below 0.5 in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a restrictive monetary policy environment, increasing borrowing costs and reducing corporate investment, which predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the 60d return correlation with FEDFUNDS drops below -0.2.
